name: soft-signup state does not fire auth_user on every turn (no-nag rule)
description:
Anti-nag guardrail. The SIGNUP_SOFT directive explicitly says "at most once per session" — the soft state should never escalate to tool-firing behaviour. This fixture sends two consecutive plain-coaching messages from an anon user in the soft window; both turns must have empty tool_uses. The hard variant (`usage_free_signup_hard_offers_auth`) is where tool firing kicks in.

Note: a strict "text-content mentions signup at most once" assertion would need the LLM-judge surface (chat-quality.spec.ts); this fixture pins the tool-trajectory side (no `auth_user` spam) which is the load-bearing safety.

parts.text: what do you mean by 'flow' in coaching?
